description: Multi-surface Phase 6: startSurface's PROCESS descriptor — first-class sugar over runShell + background, sharing the same launcher. Covers each waitUntil condition shape the descriptor accepts (httpGet against a spawned server, stdio against a REPL prompt, delayMs, and the no-waitUntil form that's ready on spawn), proves the opened surface is a real process surface (httpRequest hits the server; type drives the REPL by surface name), the tty form (PASS where the optional node-pty is installed, SKIPPED elsewhere — both fixture-legal), and closeSurface teardown. All steps are non-driver (Node only), so they run on every platform; distinct ports and names keep it safe under concurrentRunners.

tests.description: Start a background HTTP server via the process descriptor with an httpGet readiness condition, then prove it serves requests.
Start node's REPL via the process descriptor with a stdio readiness condition, then drive it by surface name with type (the surface registered under the descriptor's name is a real process surface).
The delayMs condition and the no-waitUntil form (ready as soon as the process spawns).

The tty form spawns under a pseudo-terminal. PASSes where the optional node-pty dependency is installed; lands SKIPPED (never FAIL) where it isn't — the same graceful-absence contract as runShell background.tty.
